docs.text: Back to README

docs.title: SamvadAI Docs Home
docs.text: This page gives you the quickest way to understand what SamvadAI is and how the parts fit together.

docs.title: What SamvadAI does
docs.text: SamvadAI helps complaint teams process bank complaints faster. For each complaint, it classifies category, detects sentiment, estimates priority/SLA, checks duplicate patterns, and drafts a response for agent review.

docs.title: System view
docs.text:
graph TD
    U[Bank Agent] --> W[Web App (React)]
    W --> A[API (Django-Ninja)]
    A --> DB[(SQLite)]
    A --> P[LangGraph Pipeline]
    P --> G[Gemini Cloud Model]
    P --> O[Ollama Local Model]

docs.title: Core workflow
docs.text:
1. Complaint arrives from any channel.
  2. Backend stores it as a complaint record.
  3. AI pipeline runs analysis steps.
  4. UI shows analysis + draft.
  5. Agent reviews and updates status.

docs.title: Where to go next
docs.text:
• Backend Architecture
  • Frontend Architecture
  • AI Pipeline
  • API Reference
  • Sitemap

docs.title: Honest project note
docs.text: The local demo works well. This project still needs production hardening, stronger async processing, and broader automated tests.

docs.title: AI Pipeline
docs.text: Back to README

docs.title: AI Pipeline
docs.text: This doc explains each step in the LangGraph pipeline from complaint text to drafted response.

docs.title: Pipeline graph
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Raw complaint] --> B[Classify]
    B --> C[Sentiment]
    C --> D[Priority + SLA]
    D --> E[Duplicate cluster]
    E --> F[Draft response]

docs.title: 1) Classify
docs.text: The model predicts complaint category and product area.

docs.title: 2) Sentiment
docs.text: The model labels complaint tone (for example: neutral, negative, hostile).

docs.title: 3) Priority + SLA
docs.text: The model estimates urgency and suggested SLA window.

docs.title: 4) Duplicate/cluster detection
docs.text: The pipeline checks if this complaint resembles existing issue clusters.

docs.title: 5) Draft response
docs.text: The model drafts a response text for human review.

docs.title: Model providers
docs.text:
You can switch between: - Gemini cloud (gemini-2.5-flash) - Ollama local (phi3)

Set this in environment config (for example USE_LOCAL_LLM=true).

docs.title: Why this design
docs.text: The step-based pipeline keeps logic understandable and testable. It also lets you swap a single step later without rewriting the full flow.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Back to README

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
This doc lists the main REST endpoints used by the frontend and shows simple request/response examples.

Base path: /api Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/api/docs

docs.title: Endpoint map
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A[Frontend] --> B[GET complaints list]
  A --> C[GET complaint detail by id]
  A --> D[POST analyze complaint by id]
  A --> E[POST analyze all complaints]
  A --> F[PATCH complaint status by id]
  A --> G[GET analytics summary]
  A --> H[GET analytics trends]
  A --> I[GET analytics clusters]

  D --> J[(AnalysisResult)]
  E --> J
  C --> J
  G --> K[(Aggregations)]
  H --> K
  I --> K

docs.title: GET /api/complaints/
docs.text:
List complaints, with optional filters.

Query params: - priority - status - channel

Example: 

curl "http://localhost:8000/api/complaints/?status=Open&priority=High"

docs.title: GET /api/complaints/{complaint_id}/
docs.text:
Get one complaint with optional analysis payload.

Example: 

curl "http://localhost:8000/api/complaints/<uuid>/"

docs.title: POST /api/complaints/{complaint_id}/analyze/
docs.text:
Run AI analysis for one complaint.

Example: 

curl -X POST "http://localhost:8000/api/complaints/<uuid>/analyze/"

docs.title: POST /api/complaints/analyze-all/
docs.text:
Run analysis for all complaints.

Example: 

curl -X POST "http://localhost:8000/api/complaints/analyze-all/"

docs.title: PATCH /api/complaints/{complaint_id}/status/
docs.text:
Update complaint status.

Body: 

{
  "status": "In Progress"
}

Example: 

curl -X PATCH "http://localhost:8000/api/complaints/<uuid>/status/" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status":"In Progress"}'

docs.title: GET /api/analytics/summary/
docs.text: Returns counts used by KPI cards and category/priority breakdown charts.

docs.title: GET /api/analytics/trends/
docs.text: Returns day-wise complaint counts.

docs.title: GET /api/analytics/clusters/
docs.text: Returns duplicate cluster summaries and counts.

docs.title: Response shape notes
docs.text:
• Complaint IDs are UUIDs.
  • Analysis fields can be empty when not analyzed yet.
  • Status values should follow the backend enum values.

docs.title: Backend API
docs.text: Back to README

docs.title: Backend Architecture
docs.text: This doc explains how the Django backend stores complaints, exposes APIs, and runs AI analysis.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
• Django
  • Django-Ninja (REST API + schema validation)
  • SQLite (local demo DB)
  • LangGraph pipeline integration

docs.title: Main backend folders
docs.text:
• apps/api/api/ — Django project config (settings.py, urls.py).
  • apps/api/complaints/ — models, schemas, API routes, pipeline logic.
  • apps/api/complaints/management/commands/ — seed scripts.

docs.title: Data model (high level)
docs.text:
erDiagram
    COMPLAINT ||--o| ANALYSIS_RESULT : has_one
    COMPLAINT {
      UUID id
      string customer_name
      datetime timestamp
      string channel
      string account_type
      text raw_text
      string actual_priority
      string status
    }
    ANALYSIS_RESULT {
      int id
      string category
      string product
      string sentiment
      string ai_priority
      int sla_hours
      datetime sla_deadline
      string cluster_tag
      text ai_draft
      datetime analyzed_at
    }

docs.title: API routing
docs.text:
All API endpoints are mounted under /api/ using Django-Ninja.

  • Complaint routes: listing, detail, analyze single, analyze all, status update.
  • Analytics routes: summary, trends, clusters.

See full endpoint details in API Reference.

docs.title: Local run commands
docs.text:
cd apps/api
uv sync
uv run python manage.py migrate
uv run python manage.py seed_complaints
uv run python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text:
• Bulk analysis is currently synchronous in the API path.
  • Production security settings need stricter environment setup.
  • More tests are needed around pipeline edge cases.

docs.title: Frontend Web
docs.text: Back to README

docs.title: Frontend Architecture
docs.text: This doc explains how the React app is structured and how data flows from API to UI.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
• React + Vite + TypeScript
  • shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS
  • TanStack Query (server state)
  • Zustand (client/workflow state)
  • Recharts (analytics visualizations)

docs.title: Feature areas
docs.text:
• Dashboard: KPI cards + snapshot charts.
  • Complaints: table with filtering.
  • Complaint Detail: full complaint context + AI analysis + draft editor.
  • Analytics: trend and clustering views.

docs.title: Frontend module map
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[App Layout + Router] --> B[Dashboard]
    A --> C[Complaints List]
    A --> D[Complaint Detail]
    A --> E[Analytics]

    C --> F[Filters + Table]
    D --> G[AI Analysis Panel]
    D --> H[Draft Response Editor]
    E --> I[Trend Charts]

    F --> J[API Hooks]
    G --> J
    H --> J
    I --> J
    J --> K[TanStack Query Cache]
    J --> L[Backend API]

docs.title: Data flow
docs.text:
1. Page loads.
  2. Hook calls API through shared fetcher.
  3. Query cache stores response.
  4. Components render table/cards/charts.
  5. User actions trigger mutation endpoints and invalidate stale queries.

docs.title: Page-level behavior
docs.text:
• Complaints list supports filter-based fetch.
  • Detail page retrieves complaint + analysis data.
  • Analyze actions call backend and refresh local state.
  • Analytics page reads summary/trend/cluster endpoints.

docs.title: Local run commands
docs.text:
cd apps/web
npm install
npm run dev

docs.title: Practical improvements to consider
docs.text:
• Better loading skeletons and empty states.
  • Consistent status/priority color mapping across all pages.
  • User-friendly retry UI for failed API calls.
